analysis: Hotspot is the SAXPY loop (z[i]=a*x[i]+y[i]) which is memory-bandwidth bound. Apply compiler optimization hints: ensure non-aliasing via restrict, communicate 64B alignment via assume_aligned, and add SIMD/ivdep pragmas to encourage vectorization/unrolling without changing algorithm or FP semantics. Avoid fast-math or reordering changes; keep numerical behavior stable.
selected_candidate_target: SAXPY loop vectorization and memory throughput
applied_changes_summary: Added portable restrict macro to x/y/z pointers, used __builtin_assume_aligned to provide 64-byte alignment information, and annotated the SAXPY inner loop with ivdep and simd pragmas to encourage vectorization. No algorithmic changes.
correctness_risks: If compiler does not honor alignment assumptions and pointers are not actually 64-byte aligned, may cause undefined behavior (mitigated because allocations use aligned_alloc(64,...)).
Use of restrict assumes x, y, z do not overlap; if modified in future to alias, results could change.
